## Palantir CTO warns China may be using US AI systems without authorization

A senior Palantir executive statement on adversarial AI use amplifies policy urgency around US AI export controls and could accelerate government demand for PLTR's data intelligence platforms; it also raises the possibility of regulatory action with broader AI supply-chain implications.

- Palantir CTO issued a public warning that China may be using US AI without permission.
- The warning implicates gaps in export controls and broader AI governance policy.
- Coverage frames this around unauthorized use, not a confirmed breach of a specific Palantir system.
- Story directly touches Palantir's core defense and intelligence thesis by reinforcing adversarial AI risk narratives.
